channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ex-NBA stars Carter, McGrady part of group seeking to buy stake in Buffalo Bills, AP source says
item.description: Former NBA stars Vince Carter and Tracy McGrady are part of a group being considered by the NFL to purchase a non-controlling minority stake in the Buffalo Bills.
item.dc:creator: John Wawrow And Rob Maaddi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bronny James has a left heel bruise and is listed as doubtful for the Lakers' next game
item.description: Bronny James has a bruised left heel, and he is listed as doubtful for the Los Angeles Lakers’ next game against the Utah Jazz.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pirates ace Paul Skenes and Yankees pitcher Luis Gil win Rookie of the Year awards
item.description: Hard-throwing Pittsburgh Pirates ace Paul Skenes capped a remarkable season by winning the National League Rookie of the Year Award while Luis Gil of the New York Yankees edged Baltimore’s Colton Cowser to take the AL honor.
item.dc:creator: Bernie Wilson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 4-time MLB All-Star Kris Bryant’s Lamborghini recovered after investigation cracked down on car theft ring
item.description: Colorado Rockies star third baseman Kris Bryant had his Lamborghini Huracan stolen as part of a car theft ring while reportedly trying to ship it to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mike Tyson-Jake Paul fight draws record betting for a combat sports event
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Jake Paul's unanimous victory over former heavyweight champion Mike Tyson might have been disappointing from a competitive standpoint, but it drew record betting.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Another Lakers legend to receive statue outside arena
item.description: One of the winningest coaches in NBA history will forever be enshrined in downtown Los Angeles when the Lakers unveil the newest statue on Star Plaza outside Crypto.com Arena.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Rory McIlroy brought to tears talking about rough year, including almost divorcing wife, after latest win
item.description: Rory McIlroy has had some big wins, but he was brought to tears after winning his latest trophy as he reflected on a year of adversity, including almost getting divorced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-NFL star Jason Brown left football to fight food insecurity
item.description: Jason Brown left the NFL in 2012 at the age of 29 in order to pursue a greater goal that God had in store for him.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas remains No. 1 in AP poll, top 10 shuffled with losses by 3 teams; Wisconsin joins rankings
item.description: Kansas strengthened its grip on No. 1 in the AP Top 25. The rest of the top 10 turned into a jumble of teams swapping places. The Jayhawks picked up five first-place votes in The Associated Press men's college basketball poll released on Monday, receiving 49 from a 61-person media panel. No.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southwest employee welcomes Chiefs fans by playing Bills song over intercom
item.description: She said she wanted to let Chiefs fans know that "the Buffalo Bills have the best fans in the league.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: Lions' Dan Campbell addresses 'bizarre' criticism about taking out starters in blowouts
item.description: Detroit Lions head coach Dan Campbell spoke Sunday after his decision-making process for taking out starters in the midst of blowouts after their latest win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LPGA Tour star Charley Hull appears to break out Trump's dance move during tournament
item.description: English LPGA Tour star Charley Hull appeared to join the line of professional athletes who pulled off President-elect Donald Trump's dance move over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: F1 to move Canadian Grand Prix slot on calendar in drive to cut travel
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Formula 1 will move the Canadian Grand Prix to a new slot in May from 2026 in an effort to cut down on travel and its environmental impact.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump support dominates weekend in sports as athletes perform president-elect's dance moves
item.description: Support for President-elect Donald Trump was on full display across the entire weekend as athletes performed his dance moves during matches and games.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro wrestling legend Eric Bischoff shares 1 creative angle that failed to take off
item.description: Pro wrestling legend Eric Bischoff explained to Fox News Digital one storyline that didn't get clearance to take off when he was with World Championship Wrestling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Backup QB Arbuckle leads Toronto past Winnipeg 41-24 for the Argonauts' 19th Grey Cup title
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Backup quarterback Nick Arbuckle led the Toronto Argonauts to their record-extending 19th Grey Cup title, throwing two second-half touchdown passes in a 41-24 victory over the Winnipeg Blue Bombers on Sunday night at B.C. Place.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rays will have to weather the storm next season at Steinbrenner Field
item.description: As the Tampa Bay Rays begin to gather information for their 2025 season at Steinbrenner Field in Tampa, the most unpredictable and challenging element will most likely be weather.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mountain West Conference determines there's insufficient evidence in volleyball Title IX complaint
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The Mountain West Conference has concluded there was insufficient evidence to determine misconduct between women's volleyball players from the Colorado State and San Jose State programs ahead of a match last month after a coach filed a Title IX complaint alleging the student-ath
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bears vs. Packers: Injuries, inactives, key matchups and more
item.description: CHICAGO — It's been a tumultuous several weeks for the Bears. It seems like forever since they went into the bye week feeling good with a 4-2 record. Since then, there's been a shocking Hail Mary loss in Washington and lifeless defeats to Arizona and New England.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: BYU suffers first loss of season to Kansas, 17-13
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU still has a great shot at the Big 12 Championship Game, but the Cougars undefeated season came to an end Saturday night against Kansas. Jalon Daniels threw for 169 yards, Dylan Neal ran for two touchdowns and Kansas upset No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Andrei Vasilevskiy makes 29 saves for 36th NHL shutout, Lightning beat Devils 4-0
item.description: Andrei Vasilevskiy made 29 saves for his 36th career shutout to help the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the New Jersey Devils 4-0 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN KREISER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Draymond Green's foul against Zach Edey is upgraded by the NBA to a flagrant 1
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green's foul against Memphis' Zach Edey in the third quarter of the Warriors' victory over the Grizzlies has been upgraded to a category-one flagrant, the NBA said Saturday after reviewing the play.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Donovan Mitchell scores 37, Cavaliers stay perfect and improve to 14-0 with 144-126 win over Bulls
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored a season-high 37 points, Darius Garland had 29 and the Cleveland Cavaliers continued one of the best starts in league history, improving to 14-0 with a 144-126 win over the Chicago Bulls on Friday night in an NBA Cup game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SJSU volleyball captain demands removal of transgender athlete
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (KRON) -- Some San Jose State University women's volleyball players are suing their head coach, their university, and the Division-1 Mountain West Conference for allowing a transgender player to be on the team.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: The world's tallest teenager, 7-foot-9 Olivier Rioux, plans to redshirt at Florida this season
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — The world’s tallest teenager might have to wait a year to become the world’s tallest college basketball player. Florida coach Todd Golden says Olivier Rioux, a 7-foot-9 freshman who owns a spot in the Guinness record book, is planning to redshirt.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blackhawks lose 3-1 in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Jaden Schwartz scored on a power play 1:34 into the third period, and the Seattle Kraken pushed their winning streak to three games with a 3-1 win over the Chicago Blackhawks Thursday night. Matty Beniers and Brandon Tanev also scored.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz beat Dallas for first home victory, 115-113
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was finally home sweet home for the Utah Jazz. John Collins scored the last of his 28 points on a tiebreaking dunk with 6.4 seconds left to give the Utah Jazz their first home victory of the season, 115-113 over the Dallas Mavericks on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Vasilevskiy stars as the Lightning stop the Jets' 7-game win streak with a 4-1 victory
item.description: Andrei Vasilevskiy made 24 saves in his 300th NHL win, and the Tampa Bay Lightning stopped Winnipeg’s seven-game win streak with a 4-1 victory over the Jets. Vasilevskiy is the 40th goaltender in league history to win 300 games.
item.dc:creator: JOHN KREISER, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Corner Canyon beats Lehi in OT to advance to 6A title game
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Corner Canyon is headed back to the 6A championship game, after a 35-34 overtime thriller over Lehi Thursday at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs TE Cade Otton emerging as reliable target for Baker Mayfield in absence of injured receivers
item.description: Cade Otton is showing the rest of the NFL the type of offensive weapon the Tampa Bay Buccaneers always felt he could become.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays to play 2025 season at Yankees' spring training field
item.description: The Rays' principal owner, said in an interview that Steinbrenner Field in Tampa is the best fit for the team.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Finally healthy, Puka Nacua poised for strong finish
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - Puka Nacua was not supposed to be this good, this quickly. As a fifth round draft pick out of BYU by the L.A. Rams in 2023, Nacua took the NFL by storm, setting rookie records with 104 catches for 1,486 yards.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Klay Thompson returns to cheers from some 400 Warriors employees
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Klay Thompson exited the team bus then beamed as he strolled back into Chase Center on Tuesday night, greeted by some 400 cheering Warriors employees who lined up along his path to the Dallas locker room to show their love and appreciation for the former Golden State star.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utes mired in longest losing streak in 11 years
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It has been one of the most disappointing seasons in Utah football history, and head coach Kyle Whittingham is feeling the pain. "It's been one of the most frustrating seasons of my career," Whittingham said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
